scenarios.name: Collapsed Step 4 block: capture FILES_COMMITTED before sentinel, run helper, write report with cleanup_ran=true, rm clone as FINAL action
scenarios.setup: Sandbox bare-origin + work-init clone with 3 FUSE-style staged files (data/wins.json, data/sections.json, monitor/decisions/open-issues.json). Step 4a commit creates FUSE→git commit with HEAD=that commit. Then capture FILES_COMMITTED. Then simulate Step 4c sentinel commit (HEAD advances to a 1-file commit). Then write Step 4b run-report with FILES_COMMITTED previously captured + cleanup_ran=true. Then rm -rf $CLONE.
scenarios.interpretation: The PRIMARY production fix. FILES_COMMITTED=3 (the FUSE→git commit's file count, captured BEFORE HEAD shifted to the 1-file sentinel commit) confirms Q-OP-1 answer (c) collapse keeps the variable correct without cross-bash-tool-call persistence. cleanup_ran=true is written into the JSON before rm runs (Q-OP-2 honesty trade-off accepted because rm failure mode is essentially impossible). rm -rf $CLONE at end fires reliably because it's in the same proven-invoked bash block as the run-report write. This is the failure mode that produced 3 orphans across 7 cycles per operator's 2026-05-31 diagnostic — now closed.

scenarios.name: Trap-EXIT defense-in-depth fires on mid-block failure
scenarios.setup: Subshell with trap 'rm -rf $CLONE 2>/dev/null || true' EXIT, then trigger a deliberate failure (`false`) BEFORE the explicit rm. Verify the explicit rm did NOT run but the trap did.
scenarios.interpretation: Defense in depth works. If any of the 4a/4c/4b steps throws mid-block, the EXIT trap still attempts cleanup. This was added per the directive's 'Optionally trap-protect the rm so a mid-block error still attempts cleanup'. Safe inside ONE bash tool call (does NOT fire across LLM bash sessions — different from the SKIP_LOG EXIT trap that was removed for that reason; see workspace-sync.md Operational Notes).

scenarios.name: Tinker soft-complaints grep detects cleanup_ran=false but ignores missing-field legacy reports
scenarios.setup: Three reports: legacy (no cleanup_ran field), new-with-true, new-with-false. Run a grep for cleanup_ran:false.
scenarios.interpretation: Per directive Step 4: 'Existing reports (pre-deployment) will lack the field — that's expected; tinker should treat missing as pre-deployment legacy run, ignore for runs older than the deployment timestamp.' The grep pattern '"cleanup_ran"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*false' matches only explicit false (not missing field). Tinker's Mode 2 quick-check can add: `grep -l '"cleanup_ran"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*false' monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/run-*.json` to detect leak signals.

scenarios.name: Sloppytoppy safety guard refuses rm when uncommitted changes exist
scenarios.setup: Clone with one committed file and one staged-but-not-committed file. Run the safety-guarded cleanup pattern (mirrored from analyst-baby.md lines 220-235).
scenarios.interpretation: Sloppytoppy-score and sloppytoppy-rewrite use the analyst-baby-style safety guard (`git status --porcelain | grep -q .`) before rm. Verified the guard refuses to rm when there are uncommitted changes — preserves any in-flight work that didn't make it to a push. The trap-EXIT in sloppytoppy can still fire on mid-block failure but the trap also respects the same guard (`rm -rf $CLEAN_CLONE 2>/dev/null || true` is best-effort, but the safety-guarded explicit rm is the primary).

verification_summary.primary_fix_validated: FILES_COMMITTED captured before sentinel, cleanup_ran=true written, rm fires explicitly. Confirmed via scenario 1.
verification_summary.defense_in_depth_validated: Trap-EXIT fires on mid-block failure. Confirmed via scenario 2.
verification_summary.soft_complaints_grep_validated: Tinker can distinguish cleanup_ran=false (actionable) from missing field (legacy, ignore). Confirmed via scenario 3.
verification_summary.sloppytoppy_safety_guard_validated: git status --porcelain guard preserves clones with uncommitted changes. Confirmed via scenario 4.

deployment_status: Pre-deployment test PASS. Safe to commit and push.
